title: erythraea ramosissima var. minima
intro: Erythraea ramosissima var. minima, also known as the dwarf thyme-leaved gromwell, is a rare, low-growing herbaceous perennial plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of gentiopicrin, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Erythraea ramosissima var. minima include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
